name: Current Affairs in History & National Movement (6): India's 44th UNESCO World Heritage Site & Tentative List Expansion

sourceRefs.label: usthadian.com/vajiramandravi.com - Maratha Military Landscapes (UNESCO 2025); India's 44-site UNESCO total (2026); 7 new sites added to UNESCO Tentative List

researchNotes: Sixth History current-affairs topic (6th lap). The Maratha Military Landscapes inscription directly extends Maratha Empire history (a core Part A History topic elsewhere in the project) with a genuinely recent (2025) international recognition. Expansion pass (Batch 18): added the World Heritage List vs. Tentative List distinction, a common source of exam confusion.

reading.intro: Covers India's most recent UNESCO World Heritage Site inscription and the expansion of India's UNESCO Tentative List.

qa.question: How many UNESCO World Heritage Sites does India have as of 2026, and how are they categorised?
qa.answer: 44 sites total — 36 cultural, 7 natural, and 1 mixed site — making India the 6th highest-ranked country globally for UNESCO sites.

qa.question: What was India's most recent UNESCO World Heritage Site inscription (2025), and what does it comprise?
qa.answer: The Maratha Military Landscapes — a network of 12 historic forts linked to the Maratha Empire, inscribed in 2025.

qa.question: Which site was inscribed in 2024, just before the Maratha Military Landscapes?
qa.answer: Assam's Moidams (the mound-burial system of the Ahom dynasty).

qa.question: What recent addition was made to India's UNESCO Tentative List, and what did it bring the total to?
qa.answer: 7 new natural heritage sites were added, raising India's Tentative List tally from 62 to 69. The 7 sites: Deccan Traps at Panchgani and Mahabaleshwar (Maharashtra), St. Mary's Island Cluster (Karnataka), Meghalayan Age Caves (Meghalaya), Naga Hill Ophiolite (Nagaland), Erra Matti Dibbalu (Andhra Pradesh), Natural Heritage of Tirumala Hills (Andhra Pradesh), and Varkala Cliffs (Kerala).

qa.question: What is the difference between the UNESCO 'World Heritage List' and the 'Tentative List', and why does the distinction matter for exam purposes?
qa.answer: The WORLD HERITAGE LIST contains sites FORMALLY INSCRIBED after full evaluation (India has 44). The TENTATIVE LIST is a preliminary inventory of sites a country intends to nominate in future — being on the Tentative List does NOT mean a site is already a World Heritage Site; it's a required first step before formal nomination, which can take years.

India ranks where globally in total number of UNESCO World Heritage Sites?
Being on UNESCO's 'Tentative List' means a site is:
options: Already a formally inscribed World Heritage Site
A candidate a country intends to nominate in the future
Only eligible for natural (not cultural) status
questions.explanation: A future nomination candidate, not yet inscribed.
questions.trick: Don't confuse Tentative List membership with actual World Heritage Site status — they're different stages, often years apart.
